item.title: Roche Multiple Sclerosis Drug (Fenebrutinib)
item.description:
• Summary: Roche presented data on fenebrutinib at AAN 2026, showing efficacy in delaying relapses for multiple sclerosis patients. However, liver toxicity concerns may require monitoring, impacting its competitive position against other MS therapies like Merck's IDVynso.
  • MoAs: Fenebrutinib is a Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK) inhibitor that modulates B-cell and microglial activity, reducing inflammation and neurodegeneration in multiple sclerosis.
  • Sources: BioPharma Dive, STAT News, Endpoints News

item.title: Kyverna Therapeutics (Stiff Person Syndrome)
item.description:
• Summary: Kyverna Therapeutics is advancing its therapeutic pipeline for Stiff Person Syndrome, a rare and severe neurological disorder, with approaches likely involving CAR-T cell or other immunomodulatory strategies.
  • MoAs: Kyverna's therapies may involve CAR-T cells targeting B cells or other immune modulators to address autoantibody-driven pathology in Stiff Person Syndrome.
  • Sources: STAT News
